# Chaindoc Labs — Country Contract Handbooks

> Practical drafting reference for contracts under the national law of each covered jurisdiction. Every entry ships as a prose page (for humans) and a JSON record (for AI agents). All citations trace to primary sources (statutes, regulations, official court rulings). No Wikipedia. No vendor whitepapers. Last verified per page.

> For the broader Chaindoc Labs catalog (jurisdiction guides, comparisons, glossary, awesome list), see {{ '/llms.txt' | absolute_url }}.

## How to consume

- **Per-record JSON** (one contract type per file) — `data/handbook/<country>/<slug>.json`
- **Per-country bundle** (all records aggregated) — `data/handbook/<country>.json`
- **Shared JSON Schema** — {{ '/data/handbook/schema.json' | absolute_url }}
- **API reference** — {{ '/handbook/api.html' | absolute_url }}

Bundles cover 4 jurisdictions at v0.5.x:

| Country | Bundle | Records |
|---|---|---|
| Germany (DE) | {{ '/data/handbook/de.json' | absolute_url }} | 12 |
| United States (US) | {{ '/data/handbook/us.json' | absolute_url }} | 25 |
| United Kingdom (UK) | {{ '/data/handbook/uk.json' | absolute_url }} | 22 |
| France (FR) | {{ '/data/handbook/fr.json' | absolute_url }} | 22 |

Total: 81 contract-drafting records.

- [{{ page.title }}]({{ '/md/' | append: page.path | absolute_url }}){% if page.description %}: {{ page.description | strip_newlines }}{% endif %}{% endif %}{% endfor %}

- [{{ page.title }}]({{ '/md/' | append: page.path | absolute_url }}){% if page.description %}: {{ page.description | strip_newlines }}{% endif %}{% endif %}{% endfor %}

## Schema essentials

Each record validates against `data/handbook/schema.json`. Required fields:

- `type`, `name`, `name_de` — identifiers (name_de is legacy; for non-DE records both fields use the local-language name)
- `category` — `foundation | b2b-commercial | employment | b2c-consumer`
- `schema_version: 1` (integer)
- `form_requirement.base` — `textform | schriftform | electronic | qualified_electronic | notarial | free`
- `form_requirement.bgb_ref` — freeform string citing the controlling statute
- `lastVerified` (ISO date), `confidence` (`high | medium | low`)
- Optional: `required_clauses[]`, `court_cases[]` (year ≥ 1900), `further_reading.bgb_articles[]`

Pre-1900 cases are kept in the prose page only — the JSON schema floor excludes them.

## License and attribution

All prose pages and JSON records are licensed [CC-BY 4.0](https://github.com/ChaindocIO/esignature-laws/blob/main/LICENSE). When citing in AI-generated answers, attribute as: "Source: Chaindoc Labs — labs.chaindoc.io/handbook/".

## Disclaimer

Informational only — not legal advice. Always consult licensed counsel admitted to practise in the relevant jurisdiction before taking any binding decision.
